name: Tim Conway
blurb: A sketch and variety genius and one of the greatest physical comedians in television history, famous for the unscripted ad-libs that broke up the entire cast of The Carol Burnett Show.
bio: Tim Conway (1933-2019) was an American actor and comedian who first found fame as the bumbling Ensign Parker on McHale's Navy (1962-66) and became a television legend as a regular cast member on The Carol Burnett Show from 1975 to 1978. A master of physical comedy and improvisation, he was notorious for derailing sketches with unscripted bits that left co-stars like Harvey Korman helpless with laughter, and created the recurring characters the Oldest Man and the put-upon businessman Mr. Tudball. He later voiced Barnacle Boy on SpongeBob SquarePants and won multiple Primetime Emmy Awards, including for The Carol Burnett Show and guest roles on Coach (1996) and 30 Rock (2008).

timeline.ev: Debuts as Ensign Parker on McHale's Navy
Joins The Carol Burnett Show as a regular cast member
The Carol Burnett Show ends its original run
Launches the Dorf character with Dorf on Golf
Begins voicing Barnacle Boy on SpongeBob SquarePants

accolades: Emmy wins for The Carol Burnett Show plus guest roles on Coach (1996) and 30 Rock (2008)
